# Extended Case References — Lecture K4LxKVNarnM

## Cases mentioned in passing

### German plastic injection molding machine shaft forging lap
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "German plastic injection molding machine shaft forging lap"
- **Anchor:** `§3.p4`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "How common are they? They're not very common. I actually can only think in my career of one forging lap defect that caused a problem. Well, I take that back, I can think of two. One was — the Germans had forged a 20-foot-long shaft for a plastic injection molding machine, and it had been machined and everything, went into service, and about a year and a half later had a big fatigue crack. It had a forging lap that was about an inch long and about a quarter inch deep, and that's where the fatigue crack started. I mean, you get a flaw that big in something that's rotating. Of course the Germans..."

### Kodak film digital photography failure
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Kodak film digital photography failure"
- **Anchor:** `§8.p6`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "Kodak. They were printing money. They called it film, but it was money. They had like a 95% margin on film, and they would not give up their film. They invented a lot of the best digital photography technology in the world, but the film guys didn't want to give it up because they had grown up knowing that film was their bread and butter, and they kept their bread and butter until it was all stale and rancid. So it's not just the steel industry, it's Kodak. We could go through a bunch of other industries. People — it just amazes me. These people at business schools will write articles. I..."
